name: RxJS
what_it_does: JavaScript implementation of ReactiveX (Rx), providing Observables for composing asynchronous and event-based programs using observable sequences and functional operators. RxJS Observables unify events, promises, and async iterables with a rich operator library (map, filter, mergeMap, combineLatest, debounceTime, etc.). Standard in Angular and used in any complex async coordination pattern.
use_cases: Compose complex async event streams in agent UIs — debounce user input, combine multiple data sources, cancel in-flight requests with switchMap
Handle WebSocket message streams in agents with RxJS operators for filtering, transformation, and error recovery
Coordinate multiple concurrent agent operations with combineLatest, forkJoin, or zip when results from parallel async tasks must be synchronized
Implement reactive state management in Angular-based agent UIs where component data flows are composed from observable chains
Build retry-with-backoff HTTP request pipelines using retryWhen, delay, and take operators for resilient agent API clients
not_for: Simple Promise-based async code — async/await is simpler and more readable for one-off async operations without complex composition
Teams unfamiliar with reactive programming — RxJS has a steep learning curve; over 100 operators require significant investment to use correctly
Node.js server-side code without Angular — native Promise chains or async generators are more idiomatic in Node.js without Angular's RxJS dependency
best_when: You're working with Angular, complex event stream composition, or WebSocket/SSE-based real-time data flows where reactive operators provide significant value.
avoid_when: Your team is not already using Angular or reactive patterns — async/await and Promises will be more maintainable for most server-side agent code.

auth.notes: No authentication — client-side library for async programming.

pricing.notes: RxJS is open source and free. Part of the ReactiveX family.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: RxJS errors propagate through the observable chain to the error handler. Debugging reactive pipelines is notoriously difficult — errors show the subscription stack not the operator chain. Use rxjs-spy or tag operators for debugging.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Hot vs cold observable distinction affects idempotency — cold observables restart the producer on each subscription; hot observables share a producer. Misunderstanding this is a common source of bugs.
known_agent_gotchas: Memory leaks from unsubscribed observables are the #1 RxJS bug — always unsubscribe in Angular ngOnDestroy or use takeUntil/takeUntilDestroyed pattern
Hot vs cold observables behave differently on re-subscribe — cold observables (from(promise)) restart on each subscribe; hot observables (fromEvent) share the source
switchMap cancels the previous inner observable — use mergeMap when all inner observables should complete; mixup causes silent dropped operations
Error in observable stream terminates the stream permanently — use catchError to recover; an unhandled error prevents any future emissions
RxJS 7 tree-shaking requires importing operators from 'rxjs/operators' is no longer needed — import everything from 'rxjs' directly; old import paths still work but are deprecated
firstValueFrom() and lastValueFrom() replace deprecated toPromise() in RxJS 7 — update code using .toPromise() to avoid deprecation warnings

security_score_components.security_notes: Local library. Observable chains can be used to process sensitive data — ensure operators don't log or cache sensitive values unintentionally.
